title: Context Assembly, Trust Labels, Token Budgets, Truncation, and Compaction · Spiral Review
summary: A delayed engineering review reconstructs context assembly, trust labels, token budgets, truncation, and compaction from stakeholder, revision, runtime, model, interaction, call, result, state, approval, evaluation, trace, failure, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for budgeted provenance-aware context builder after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Label each context item by source, authority, tenant, purpose, timestamp, sensitivity, trust, instruction status, and expiry.
Reserve output and tool budget, measure or conservatively estimate input tokens, and admit context by task-specific value and risk.
Preserve active policy, user goal, approvals, tool schemas, recent results, unresolved errors, and evidence while removing redundant or expired material.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-ai-agent-python cumulative safe evaluated AI agent portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python interaction, tool, policy, state, memory,
evaluation, telemetry, and recovery models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only.
Provider APIs, Gemini models, native google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, credentials,
host files, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, cost, privacy review,
and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentPolicy:
    stakeholder_task_id: str
    allowed_tool_names: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_steps: int = 12
    maximum_tool_calls: int = 8
    approval_required_for_writes: bool = True


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentEvidence:
    revision: str
    model_config_digest: str
    policy_digest: str
    evaluation_digest: str
    recovery_drill_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Safe evaluated Python AI agent portfolio ready"

options.text: For separate evidence from confidence, run one convenient fluent model response, omit stakeholder risk, revision, model and API identity, interaction and call correlation, tool schema and authority, state and resource budgets, approval, changed fixtures, injection, privacy, accessibility, evaluation, recovery, and controlled provider transfer, then accept plausible text as agent proof. This does not satisfy Compaction Regression for budgeted provenance-aware context builder.
The record names the assumption, applies Compaction Regression, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Compaction Regression record says budgeted provenance-aware context builder looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Compaction Regression.

steps.title: Safe AI agents and Python Record what required effort · Review Compaction Regression
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Compaction Regression: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review agent engineering team handles case 5474 during a keyboard and screen-reader interaction review. Build deterministic Python evidence to assemble context by relevance, authority, provenance, recency, token budget, compaction loss tests, and explicit untrusted labels; loops, concurrency, tokens, time, retries, and spend stay below explicit budgets; then replace trusted context with untrusted indirect instructions and preserve data-versus-instruction boundaries. Browser code uses pure bounded state machines and original fixed fixtures only. Real provider APIs, Gemini models, google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic native validation, HTTP, files, credentials, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, privacy review, and production effects require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Test pre- and post-compaction decisions on changed goals, permissions, failures, tool calls, citations, and adversarial instructions.

steps.why: Retrieving Compaction Regression through the concrete budgeted provenance-aware context builder case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test pre- and post-compaction decisions on changed goals, permissions, failures, tool calls, citations, and adversarial instructions.
